Notes: Agent-assisted time is 24% of manual baseline, well under the 50% target. Human review time is the dominant component — further prompt quality improvements would reduce review time at checkpoints.

Agent Execution Time: 8 min (API calls to Salesforce, Zendesk, Mixpanel, Slack + data structuring)
Human Review Time: 10 min (verify data completeness and accuracy against known account state)
Notes: Largest absolute time saving. Manual data gathering involves logging into 4 systems, running queries/exports, and compiling into a working document. The agent handles all API calls and data structuring; human review is a quick verification that the right data was pulled.

Metric: Analysis (health scoring, risk assessment, trend analysis)
Agent Execution Time: 3 min (LLM calls for usage analysis, ticket analysis, sentiment analysis, synthesis)
Human Review Time: 25 min (review health scores, verify risk flags, check trend interpretations against manual judgement)
Notes: Agent produces analysis drafts that require review but not recreation. Human review focuses on verifying scores and risk flags match manual judgement rather than performing the analysis from scratch. Post-iteration-2, multi-product analysis review time at this stage dropped (per-product breakdowns eliminate manual annotation).

Agent Execution Time: 1 min (LLM call for report generation from structured analysis)
Human Review Time: 25 min (review narrative quality, edit wording, verify recommendations are specific and actionable)
Notes: Report generation is fast (single LLM call); the human time is in editing for tone, specificity, and professional standard. Minor quality gaps (e.g., TC1 generic exec summary) are addressed through wording edits rather than structural rewrites. Quality improves as prompts are refined across iteration cycles.

Manual Process Time: N/A (no equivalent in manual process — analysis is done by the human)

Target: Checkpoint edits should be none/minor in > 80% of cases (indicating the analysis action is doing its job)
Notes: Post-iteration, edits at this checkpoint are consistently none or minor tweaks across all 5 test cases. Pre-iteration, TC2 required significant corrections (sentiment) and TC5 required complete rewrite (per-product). The shift from substantive corrections to minor tweaks confirms the underlying fixes addressed root causes.
